2603.11594.title: Leveraging Large Language Models and Survival Analysis for Early Prediction of Chemotherapy Outcomes
2603.11594.paper_summary: Chemotherapy for cancer treatment is costly and accompanied by severe side effects, highlighting the critical need for early prediction of treatment outcomes to improve patient management and informed decision-making. Predictive models for chemotherapy outcomes using real-world data face challenges, including the absence of explicit phenotypes and treatment outcome labels such as cancer progression and toxicity. This study addresses these challenges by employing Large Language Models (LLMs) and ontology-based techniques for phenotypes and outcome label extraction from patient notes. We focused on one of the most frequently occurring cancers, breast cancer, due to its high prevalence and significant variability in patient response to treatment, making it a critical area for improving predictive modeling. The dataset included features such as vitals, demographics, staging, biomarkers, and performance scales. Drug regimens and their combinations were extracted from the chemotherapy plans in the EMR data and shortlisted based on NCCN guidelines, verified with NIH standards, and analyzed through survival modeling. The proposed approach significantly reduced phenotypes sparsity and improved predictive accuracy. Random Survival Forest was used to predict time-to-failure, achieving a C-index of 73%, and utilized as a classifier at a specific time point to predict treatment outcomes, with accuracy and F1 scores above 70%. The outcome probabilities were validated for reliability by calibration curves. We extended our approach to four other cancer types. This research highlights the potential of early prediction of treatment outcomes using LLM-based clinical data extraction enabling personalized treatment plans with better patient outcomes.
2603.11594.paper_summary_zh: 化療治療癌症的成本高昂，並伴隨著嚴重的副作用，突顯了早期預測治療結果以改善病人管理和知情決策的關鍵需求。使用真實世界數據的化療結果預測模型面臨挑戰，包括缺乏明確的表型和治療結果標籤，如癌症進展和毒性。本研究通過使用大型語言模型（LLMs）和基於本體的技術來從病人筆記中提取表型和結果標籤，來解決這些挑戰。我們專注於最常見的癌症之一——乳腺癌，因為其高發病率和病人對治療反應的顯著變異，使其成為改善預測建模的關鍵領域。數據集包含了生命體徵、人口統計、分期、生物標誌物和表現量表等特徵。從電子病歷數據中的化療計劃提取了藥物方案及其組合，並根據NCCN指南進行篩選，並通過NIH標準進行驗證，並通過生存模型進行分析。所提出的方法顯著減少了表型的稀疏性並提高了預測準確性。隨機生存森林被用來預測失敗時間，達到73%的C指數，並在特定時間點作為分類器來預測治療結果，準確率和F1分數均超過70%。結果概率通過校準曲線進行了可靠性驗證。我們將我們的方法擴展到其他四種癌症類型。本研究突顯了使用基於LLM的臨床數據提取來早期預測治療結果的潛力，使個性化治療計劃能夠實現更好的病人結果。
2603.11594.author: Muhammad Faisal Shahid et.al.
2603.11594.authors: Muhammad Faisal Shahid, Asad Afzal, Abdullah Faiz, Muhammad Siddiqui, Arbaz Khan Shehzad, Fatima Aftab, Muhammad Usamah Shahid, Muddassar Farooq
